audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for persecution warning, Paraclete/Spirit of truth, conviction, guiding into truth, grief-to-joy birth imagery, asking in name, plain speech, peace/tribulation.

verses.restored: These things I have spoken to you so that you may not be made to stumble.
They will put you out of synagogues; indeed an hour is coming when everyone who kills you will think to offer service to the Divine.
They will do these things because they did not know the Father nor me.
But I have spoken these things to you so that when their hour comes, you may remember that I told you. I did not say these things to you from the beginning because I was with you.
Now I go to the One who sent me, and none of you asks me, Where are you going?
But because I have spoken these things to you, grief has filled your heart.
But I tell you the truth: it is advantageous for you that I go away. For if I do not go away, the Advocate/Paraclete will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
When that one comes, he will expose/convict the world concerning sin/misalignment, concerning righteousness/justice, and concerning judgment.
Concerning sin/misalignment, because they do not trust into me.
Concerning righteousness/justice, because I go to the Father and you behold me no longer.
Concerning judgment, because the ruler of this world has been judged.
I still have many things to say to you, but you are not able to bear them now.
But when that one comes, the Spirit of truth, he will guide you into all truth; for he will not speak from himself, but whatever he hears he will speak, and the coming things he will announce to you.
That one will glorify me, because he will receive from what is mine and announce it to you.
All things that the Father has are mine; because of this I said that he receives from what is mine and announces it to you.
A little while, and you behold me no longer; and again a little while, and you will see me.

Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, What is this he says to us, A little while and you do not behold me, and again a little while and you will see me, and, Because I go to the Father?
They were saying, What is this little while? We do not know what he speaks.
Jesus knew they wanted to ask him, and said to them, Are you seeking with one another about this, that I said, A little while and you do not behold me, and again a little while and you will see me?
Amen, amen, I say to you: you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice; you will be grieved, but your grief will become joy.
A woman, when she gives birth, has grief because her hour has come; but when the child is born, she no longer remembers the pressure because of joy that a human has been born into the world.
So you also now have grief; but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one takes your joy from you.
In that day you will ask me nothing. Amen, amen, I say to you: whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give you.
Until now you have asked nothing in my name; ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be fulfilled.
These things I have spoken to you in figures; an hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in figures, but will announce to you plainly concerning the Father.
In that day you will ask in my name, and I do not say to you that I will ask the Father concerning you,
for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have trusted that I came from the Divine.
I came out from the Father and have come into the world; again I leave the world and go to the Father.
His disciples say, Look, now you speak plainly and speak no figure.
Now we know that you know all things and have no need that anyone question you; by this we trust that you came from the Divine.
Jesus answered them, Do you now trust?
Look, an hour is coming, and has come, that you will be scattered, each to one's own, and you will leave me alone; yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
These things I have spoken to you so that in me you may have peace. In the world you have pressure/tribulation; but take courage: I have overcome the world.
